name: BeamKit Foundation Safety Registry
description: PHI-free starter hazard and control registry for research, shadow validation, and clinical-pilot planning.

hazards.title: False pass
hazards.hazardousSituation: A non-compliant treatment plan is reported as passing automated validation.
hazards.potentialHarm: A reviewer may miss a protocol, policy, prescription, structure, or dose issue during plan review.

hazards.title: Wrong protocol or rule pack
hazards.hazardousSituation: A plan is evaluated against an incorrect, stale, retired, or locally unapproved protocol.
hazards.potentialHarm: The clinical team may rely on validation findings that do not match the intended treatment protocol.

hazards.title: Stale plan snapshot
hazards.hazardousSituation: Validation uses an exported plan snapshot that no longer matches the treatment-planning-system plan.
hazards.potentialHarm: A changed prescription, dose, structure, beam, or QA plan may not be detected before treatment readiness.

hazards.title: Protected health information exposure
hazards.hazardousSituation: PHI is committed, logged, exported, retained, or exposed through plan artifacts.
hazards.potentialHarm: Patient privacy may be compromised and the deployment may violate institutional policy or law.

controls.title: Requirement traceability
controls.description: Clinical rules and plan checks must link to a stable requirement id, source reference, rationale, hazard id, and safety-control id before clinical-pilot promotion.

controls.title: Regression testing
controls.description: Known-pass, known-fail, edge-case, and protocol-specific cases must pass before a rule pack is promoted.

controls.title: Qualified human review
controls.description: BeamKit outputs remain advisory and must be reviewed by qualified clinical staff before any clinical decision.

controls.title: Clinical review signoff
controls.description: A qualified clinical or physics reviewer accepted the rule pack, RT-PX acceptance, or validation evidence for the stated intended use.

controls.title: Approval-gated protocol promotion
controls.description: RT-PX packages and rule packs must carry reviewer, approver, effective-date, rationale, and source-reference metadata before promotion.

controls.title: Plan and policy provenance
controls.description: Validation reports must record plan, prescription, source snapshot, rule-pack, and protocol fingerprints.

controls.title: Change detection
controls.description: Plan write-up and QA evidence must be marked stale when key plan, prescription, dose, structure, beam, or rule-pack fields change.

controls.title: Periodic policy review
controls.description: Clinical content must declare a review due date and block or warn when the owner has not completed re-review.

controls.title: RT-PX package accepted
controls.description: The package accepted without blocking structure, validation, or optional ESAPI evidence errors.

controls.title: Generated RT-PX rule-pack regression tests
controls.description: The generated rule pack was tested against BeamKit synthetic regression cases before promotion.

controls.title: RT-PX local clinical acceptance review
controls.description: The treating institution completed local review metadata before promoting the generated rule pack.

controls.title: Optional RT-PX ESAPI evidence evaluated
controls.description: The acceptance workflow compared the package against an ESAPI-exported plan snapshot when supplied.

controls.title: PHI-free open repository
controls.description: Repository samples, tests, and documentation must use synthetic or de-identified data only.

controls.title: Access control
controls.description: Clinical deployments must restrict APIs, dashboards, uploads, retained artifacts, and audit data to authorized users.

controls.title: Audit logging
controls.description: Clinical deployments must record who submitted, reviewed, accepted, promoted, or varied clinical validation artifacts.
